build.actual: Both required builds passed after installing missing sandbox dependencies: Rust 1.92.0 via rustup, protobuf-compiler/pkg-config, warp-channel-config helper, and X11/GL runtime packages.

app_launch.method: Launched built Warp app on DISPLAY=:100 using a fresh Xvfb server with GLX/llvmpipe; authenticated with STAGING_USER_WARP_API_KEY; isolated HOME/XDG_* under /tmp/warpctrl-verify.

permission_setup.settings: Fresh isolated profile; outside-Warp local control default false.
permission_setup.discovery: Live discovery record had outside_warp_control_enabled=false and endpoint=null.

permission_setup.settings: Preseeded private preferences equivalent to Settings > Scripting toggles: LocalControlAllowOutsideWarp=true and LocalControlOutsideWarpMetadataReads=true; LocalControlOutsideWarpUnderlyingDataReads unset/false; relaunched Warp.
permission_setup.discovery: Live discovery record advertised an outside-Warp endpoint.

permission_setup.settings: Preseeded LocalControlOutsideWarpUnderlyingDataReads=true in addition to outside-Warp control and metadata reads; relaunched Warp.
permission_setup.discovery: Live discovery record advertised an outside-Warp endpoint.

ui_notes: The Settings UI was available visually, but reliable X input was impeded by first-run onboarding/agent focus in the headless Xvfb environment. The same private keys toggled by Settings > Scripting were preseeded in the isolated profile and Warp was relaunched between phases.

commands.expected_result: Denied because outside-Warp control is default-off.
commands.actual_result: Denied with local_control_disabled: outside-Warp local control credential broker is disabled for this instance.

commands.expected_result: Denied because outside-Warp underlying-data reads are disabled while outside control is enabled.
commands.actual_result: Denied with insufficient_permissions: block.inspect requires a local-control permission that is disabled.

commands.expected_result: Return visible Warp windows to help diagnose selectors.
commands.actual_result: Succeeded; returned one window with title New agent conversation and window_id 0.

commands.invocation_context: outside_warp from external xterm; Warp window refocused during invocation
commands.expected_result: Return block metadata because block.list is categorized as metadata read, not underlying-data read.
commands.actual_result: Succeeded; returned two blocks with block ids precmd-17797603471627-0 and precmd-17797603471627-1.

commands.expected_result: Return block metadata if Warp is the active window.
commands.actual_result: Failed with missing_target because the screenshot xterm stole active-window focus; superseded by focus-coordinated success screenshot.

commands.expected_result: Check whether explicit window selectors can avoid focus dependency.
commands.actual_result: Failed with invalid_selector: block.list only supports the active window selector.

commands.invocation_context: outside_warp from external xterm; Warp window refocused during invocation
commands.expected_result: Return block details after underlying-data-read permission is enabled.
commands.actual_result: Failed with unsupported_action: block.inspect is not implemented by this local-control bridge. This conflicts with catalog metadata that reports block.inspect implementation_status=implemented.

commands.invocation_context: outside_warp from external xterm; Warp window refocused during invocation
commands.expected_result: Return block output after underlying-data-read permission is enabled.
commands.actual_result: Succeeded; returned block metadata and an empty plain output string for the selected restored precmd block.

commands.invocation_context: outside_warp from external xterm; Warp window refocused during invocation
commands.expected_result: Return staged input buffer after underlying-data-read permission is enabled.
commands.actual_result: Succeeded; returned session_id 177976048613780, empty text, cursor_offset 0.

commands.invocation_context: outside_warp from external xterm; Warp window refocused during invocation
commands.expected_result: Return command history after underlying-data-read permission is enabled.
commands.actual_result: Succeeded; returned an empty entries array for the disposable/restored session state.

commands.required_permission_category: none for local catalog; action metadata category is read_underlying_data
commands.actual_result: Succeeded; catalog reports implementation_status=implemented, risk_tier=read_only_terminal_data, state_data_category=underlying_data_read, permission_category=read_underlying_data.

findings: Default-off outside-Warp behavior is verified: no broker/endpoint is exposed and underlying-data reads fail closed.
With outside-Warp control enabled but underlying-data reads disabled, block.inspect fails with insufficient_permissions as expected.
With underlying-data reads enabled, block.output, input.get, and history.list succeed in JSON mode.
block.inspect is advertised by the catalog as implemented but the app bridge returns unsupported_action after permissions are enabled; this should be reviewed before declaring the slice ready.
block.list is categorized as read_metadata rather than read_underlying_data; it succeeds when metadata reads are enabled and returns block ids/session ids without enabling underlying-data reads.

block.list currently requires the active Warp window selector; external terminals used for screenshots steal focus unless Warp is explicitly refocused before invocation. Explicit --window-index 0 is rejected for block.list.
